Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-26-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Richard A. Hoyer, Esq., for All Day Homecare, Inc., Plaintiff/Moving Party
Frank S. Moore, Esq., for Serafina Buresove, Defendant/Opposing Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff All Day Homecare, Inc.'s Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And For Sanctions is denied as the motion was not made within the 45 day time period required under the Code of Civil Procedure. The motion made on November 28, 2006 was of no effect as all matters regarding discovery were stayed. An action that could not have been adjudicated because of a stay has no legal effect. The motion had to have been filed by January 29, 2007. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
FRANK S. MOORE, 292-6091, APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY
RICHARD HOYER, 956-1360, APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF AND RESPONDING PARTY

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DONALD MCCRAE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. TRIABLE ISSUES REMAIN AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAD A PROTECTIBLE INTEREST IN THE IDENTITY OF ITS CLIENT AND EMPLOYEE SUCH THAT THERE MAY HAVE BEEN A TRADE SECRET, WHICH, IN TURN, COULD JUSTIFY THE RELATIVELY LIMITED RESTRAINT IN PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT WITH BURESOVA AND AGREEMENT WITH MCCRAE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. (301/PJB/JF) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189
